_description: OKR (Objectives and Key Results) goal-setting and strategic alignment framework. Tracks objectives, key results, check-ins, initiatives, and OKR cycles. Includes reactive computations for progress, status, and rollup scoring. Two processes: OKR Planning (Tier 1) and OKR Review (Tier 2 with obligations). Four intelligence artifacts for attainment, alignment, compliance, and initiative effort analysis.
_organizations_note: Organizations defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.
_tenants_note: Tenants defined in app_erp_base. Load app_erp_base first.
_erp_goal_note: erp-goal universal and its 11 properties (erp-goal-title-prop through erp-goal-score-prop) are defined in app_erp_base. erp-objective and erp-key-result generalize erp-goal from the base app.

description.en: Status of any goal or key result: Not Started, On Track, At Risk, Behind, Achieved, Missed
Organizational level at which an objective is set: Company, Business Unit, Department, Team, Individual
How the key result is measured: Metric, Milestone, Binary
Owner subjective confidence in achieving the goal: High, Medium, Low
Lifecycle status of an initiative: Planned, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled, Blocked
Method used to calculate the final score: Percentage, Binary, Milestone, Weighted
Lifecycle status of an OKR cycle: Planning, Active, Review, Closed
Qualitative strategic objective. Expresses intent (e.g. Become the market leader in X). Composes key results.
Quantitative measurable outcome. Expresses a number to hit (e.g. Increase revenue from X to Y).
Time-bounded OKR period, typically quarterly. Contains objectives and drives the review cadence.
Periodic progress update on a key result. Records current value, confidence, and notes at a point in time.
Action plan to achieve a key result. Has a lifecycle (planned, in progress, completed).
Materialized aggregate for OKR attainment artifact definition
Materialized aggregate for OKR alignment health artifact definition
Materialized aggregate for check-in compliance artifact definition
Materialized aggregate for initiative effort artifact definition
Accountable for an objective outcomes. Typically a manager or team lead. Reviews KR progress, facilitates alignment, signs off on scoring.
Responsible for delivering a specific key result. Updates progress via check-ins, maintains confidence assessments, owns the number.
Drives the OKR process across the organization. Facilitates planning sessions, reviews, and retrospectives. Ensures consistency and quality of OKRs.

universals._comment: === RELATOR: Objective has Key Results (aggregation -- KRs are authored content) ===

description.en: Objective aggregates its key results. KRs are authored semantic children, not fixed structural components. Typical cardinality: 3-5 KRs per objective.

universals._comment: === RELATOR: Key Result has Check-Ins (composition -- check-ins are structural) ===

description.en: Check-ins belong to a key result. Deletion cascades. A check-in has no meaning outside its KR.

universals._comment: === RELATOR: Key Result has Initiatives (association -- independent lifecycle) ===

description.en: Initiative supports a KR but has an independent lifecycle. Many-to-many: one initiative may support multiple KRs.
Cascading alignment. Child objectives align to parent objectives. Creates the cascade: Company > BU > Department > Team > Individual.
Objective belongs to a cycle. Each objective exists in exactly one cycle. Enables cycle-scoped analytics.

universals._comment: === PROCESS: OKR Planning (Tier 1 -- 3 stages, no obligations) ===
universals._note_cross_app_relators: SKIPPED cross-app relators: erp-cycle-quarter-rel (Fiscal Calendar), erp-objective-orgunit-owner-rel (Org Structure), erp-objective-person-owner-rel (Party Master), erp-kr-owner-rel (Party Master), erp-initiative-owner-rel (Party Master), erp-initiative-project-rel (Project Management), erp-kr-budget-rel (Budget). These reference universals from other apps and should be defined when those apps are deployed alongside OKR.

description.en: Planning process for each OKR cycle. 3 stages: Draft, Alignment, Finalization. No formal obligations (Tier 1).
Objectives and KRs are drafted by owners. Brainstorming, wordsmithing, target-setting.
Cascading alignment reviewed. Parent-child objective links validated. Cross-team dependencies identified.
Final review. Objectives are locked. Cycle status transitions to Active.

universals._comment: === PROCESS: OKR Review (Tier 2 -- 3 stages with obligations) ===

description.en: Review process at end of each cycle. 3 stages with obligations: Self-Assessment (KR Owner), Manager Review (Objective Owner), Scoring (OKR Champion).
KR owners update final progress values, write retrospective notes, and self-score their KRs.

universals._comment: === STAGE: Manager Review (review stage 2 with obligation) ===

description.en: Objective owners review team OKRs, validate scores, add manager commentary, and flag discrepancies.
OKR champion finalizes scores, conducts retrospective, documents learnings, and transitions cycle to Closed.
Obligation for KR owners to update final progress values and self-score their KRs.
Obligation for objective owners to review team OKRs, validate scores, and add manager commentary.
Obligation for OKR champion to finalize scores, conduct retrospective, and close cycle.

universals._comment: === COMPOSITION RELATOR: Planning Process -> Draft Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Planning Process -> Alignment Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Planning Process -> Finalization Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Review Process -> Self-Assessment Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Review Process -> Manager Review Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Review Process -> Scoring Stage ===
=== COMPOSITION RELATOR: Manager Review Stage -> Obligation ===

description.en: True if moonshot objective. Aspirational objectives use adjusted scoring: 70% achievement = on track.
Computed: weighted average of child KR scores.
Unit of measurement for display (%, $, count, users, etc.).
Computed: average goal attainment across all objectives in the cycle.
Computed: change from previous check-in value.
Estimated person-days of effort.
Actual person-days spent.
Percentage complete (0-100).
Emitted when a KR progress reaches 100% and status is set to Achieved
Emitted when KR status changes to At Risk (progress 40-70%)
Emitted when KR status changes to Behind (progress < 40%)
Emitted when cycle status transitions to Closed in the scoring stage
Emitted by timer when a KR has not been checked in for > 14 days
Artifact threshold: avg KR score < 40 for an objective level
Artifact threshold: alignment ratio < 70% for an objective level
Artifact threshold: check-in compliance rate < 50%

timers.name: Check-In Reminder
timers._comment: Weekly timer that checks all active KRs for overdue check-ins (> 14 days since last check-in)

timers.name: Cycle Close Trigger
timers._comment: Daily timer that checks if any cycle has passed its end date and should transition to Review

description.en: Computes progress percentage based on current, target, baseline values and KR type (Metric/Binary/Milestone)

computations.formula: let t = target.value; let c = current.value; let b = baseline.value; let range = t - b; let p = if range == 0.0 { 100.0 } else { ((c - b) / range) * 100.0 }; if p < 0.0 { 0.0 } else if p > 100.0 { 100.0 } else { p }

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 2: KR Status Auto-Update (progress -> status category) ===

description.en: Sets KR status based on progress: >= 100 Achieved, >= 70 On Track, >= 40 At Risk, > 0 Behind, else Not Started

computations.formula: let p = progress.value; if p >= 100.0 { 5.0 } else if p >= 70.0 { 4.0 } else if p >= 40.0 { 3.0 } else if p > 0.0 { 2.0 } else { 1.0 }

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 3: Objective Score Rollup (weighted avg of child KR scores) ===

description.en: Computes objective score as weighted average of child KR scores via erp-objective-has-kr-rel

computations.formula: let s = kr_score_sum.value; let c = kr_count.value; if c > 0.0 { s / c } else { 0.0 }

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 4: Check-In Delta (change from previous check-in) ===

description.en: Computes delta between current and previous check-in value. First check-in uses 0 as baseline.

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 5: Cycle Average Attainment (avg objective scores in cycle) ===

description.en: Computes average goal score across all objectives in the cycle via erp-objective-cycle-rel

computations.formula: let s = obj_score_sum.value; let c = obj_count.value; if c > 0.0 { s / c } else { 0.0 }

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 6: Check-In to KR Value Propagation ===

description.en: Propagates latest check-in value to parent KR current value via erp-kr-has-checkin-rel. Triggers KR progress cascade.

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 1: OKR Attainment by Objective Level ===
artifact_definitions.name: OKR Attainment by Objective Level

description.en: Tracks key result achievement rates across objective levels and cycles. Enables leadership to identify which tiers are executing well.
Measures how well objectives are aligned in the cascade hierarchy. Unaligned (orphan) objectives indicate a breakdown in strategic coherence.
Tracks whether KR owners are submitting regular progress updates. Low compliance is the leading indicator of OKR program failure.
Aggregates initiative effort (estimated and actual person-days) by status. Enables resource planning and estimation accuracy analysis.

OKR goal-setting and strategic alignment framework. Tracks objectives, key results, check-ins, initiatives, and OKR cycles with reactive computations, two processes (Planning and Review), and four intelligence artifacts.
Core OKR operations: objectives, key results, check-ins, initiatives, cycles
Intelligence artifacts, attainment analysis, alignment health, compliance monitoring
OKR planning and review processes with stage-based workflows
